title: Senior AI Engineer – Agentic Platform Lead
company_focus: Defense, national security, and space technology
description:
Voyager is an innovative defense, national security and
space technology company committed to advancing and
delivering transformative, mission-critical solutions. We
tackle the most complex challenges to unlock new frontiers
for human progress, fortify national security, and protect
critical assets to lead in the race for technological and
operational superiority from ground to space.

Forge the Future: Join Voyager Technologies

The future belongs to those who build it. At Voyager
Technologies, we’re building technologies that protect
lives, expand frontiers and prepare us for what’s next. And
we’re doing that with people who are wired to solve, build,
adapt and lead. These roles are not for the faint of heart. 

You’ll help lay the foundation for humanity's future. Join a
culture where innovation thrives, curiosity is rewarded, and
impact is real. We’re a company of doers, thinkers and
builders, united by purpose and grounded in reality.

If you want to put your skills to work where the stakes are
real and the mission is bigger than any one person, forge
the future with Voyager.

____________________________________________________________
________________________

Job Summary:

We are seeking a Senior AI Engineer – Agentic Platform Lead
to serve as one of the senior technical leaders for the
Agentic Computational Engineering (ACE) team. In this role,
you will architect and build the multi-agent platform,
execution environment, and core services that allow AI
agents to design complex hardware systems end-to-end.

You will own the technical roadmap for the ACE platform,
lead a small team of exceptional engineers, and partner
closely with domain experts across propulsion, electronics,
and manufacturing. This role is ideal for a builder who is
obsessed with velocity, comfortable in ambiguity, and
excited to spend their days turning cutting-edge AI research
into hard industrial capability. You are the kind of
engineer who uses AI as leverage, not a novelty, and who
views waiting on slow tools as a bug in the system to be
eliminated.

You will be joining the Agentic Computational Engineering
(ACE) team, a specialized group within our Advanced
Technology Development organization. ACE is responsible for
building Voyager’s Generative Engine – the AI-native
platform that compresses complex hardware development cycles
from years to days. We design agentic AI systems that pair
deeply with physics simulation, test data, and modern
manufacturing so that Design for Manufacturing (DfM), Design
for Assembly (DfA), and Design for Test (DfT) are built into
the very first line of code and the very first sketch of a
design.

Responsibilities:

- Architect and own the full stack ACE platform for the
  Generative Engine, including agent orchestration,
  execution runtimes, and frontend and backend services.
- Design and implement distributed systems patterns (e.g.,
  orchestrator–worker, swarms) that coordinate AI agents
  across the full hardware design lifecycle.
- Build the secure execution layer where agents can
  autonomously run code, simulations, and analyses against
  internal tools and data sources.
- Design and implement memory, context, and provenance systems
  (RAG, vector stores, decision logs) so agents can recall
  and justify prior design decisions.
- Aggressively profile and optimize system performance, with a
  focus on latency, reliability, and observability for large
  numbers of concurrent agents.
- Lead and mentor a small team of exceptional engineers,
  scientists, and/or mathematicians setting standards,
  reviewing code, and growing early-career talent.
- Collaborate with domain teams (electronics, propulsion,
  manufacturing) to translate their workflows into scalable
  agentic capabilities on the platform.
- Influence long-range technical strategy for ACE and help
  define how agentic AI is used across Voyager’s engineering
  stack.

Required Qualifications:

- Master’s degree + 5 years, or PhD + 3 years, in Computer
  Science, Physics, Mathematics, or a related technical
  field; or equivalent practical experience.
- 5+ years of experience building production-grade backend or
  distributed systems in high-throughput or mission-critical
  environments.
- Deep experience with modern programming languages such as
  Python, Go, or Rust, and with microservice architectures,
  message buses, and containerization.
- Hands-on experience building or integrating LLM-based
  systems, including agent orchestration frameworks (e.g.,
  LangGraph, AutoGen, custom runtimes).
- Demonstrated “AI-first” workflow: you routinely use LLMs and
  agents to accelerate coding, documentation, and testing
  and have examples of where this 10×’d your output.
- Proven ability to lead technical projects, mentor other
  engineers, and make architectural decisions in a fast-
  paced, highly ambiguous environment.

Preferred Qualifications:

- Background or minor in Mechanical, Aerospace, Electrical
  Engineering, Physics, Chemistry, or Applied Mathematics.
- Experience designing systems that connect AI agents to
  engineering tools (e.g., Ansys, Altium, CAD/CAE platforms)
  and internal services.
- Experience with observability stacks (logging, tracing,
  metrics) for complex distributed systems.
- Prior experience building platforms that support safety-
  critical or mission-critical applications at companies
  like SpaceX.
- A track record of personal projects or open-source work that
  shows your passion for building systems, not just doing a
  job.

Voyager offers a comprehensive, total compensation package,
which includes competitive salary, a discretionary annual
bonus plan, paid time off (PTO), a comprehensive health
benefit package, retirement savings, wellness program, and
various other benefits. When you join our team, you’re not
just an employee; you become part of a dynamic community
dedicated to innovation and excellence.

To conform to U.S. Government space technology export
regulations, including the International Traffic in Arms
Regulations (ITAR) you must be a U.S. citizen, lawful
permanent resident of the U.S., protected individual as
defined by 8 U.S.C. 1324b(a)(3), or eligible to obtain the
required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State.

Voyager is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment
decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion,
national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender
identity or expression, age, disability, protected veteran
status or other characteristics protected by law. 

Minority/Female/Disabled/Veteran

The statements contained in this job description are
intended to describe the general content and requirements
for performance of this job. It is not intended to be an
exhaustive list of all job duties, responsibilities, and
requirements. This job description is not an employment
agreement or contract. Management has the exclusive right to
alter the scope of work within the framework of this job
description at any time without prior notice.

$165,000—$250,000 USD

ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves directly building and architecting multi-
agent AI systems, implementing LLM-based agents, and
creating agent orchestration frameworks. The engineer is
hands-on with agentic AI platforms, RAG systems, and
autonomous agent execution environments for hardware design
automation.
